pages.full_text:
Friday we set off about 9.45 or 10, after filling w/ water + emptying loo.

Paray-le-Monial

[three photos of church/abbey interior]

Paray-le-Monial (Saône-et-Loire) Burgundy
Christ in Glory, North Portal, [?Crche]
photo_captions: Paray-le-Monial (Saône-et-Loire) Burgundy Christ in Glory, North Portal, [?Crche]
pages.notes: The last word of the photo caption reads '[?Crche]' — possibly an abbreviated 'Creche', 'Crèche', or 'Porche'. No handwritten page number visible. The heading 'Paray-le-Monial' appears to be a printed/typed label on a photo or postcard rather than handwritten. The page date 'Friday' cannot be anchored to a year from this page alone; context from surrounding pages suggests this is part of a summer trip (August). Three photographs show church/abbey interiors.

pages.full_text:
off to Intermarché, [?Mouskatère], better than he Clerke earlier in the week. Rabbit pâté good. We had lunch on a Canal, le Canal lateral de la Loire, next to an Abbey. On to Paray-le-Monial. This has many women's religious orders represented. Simon didn't come into one of the most interesting churches with this theme because it had a "no shorts" sign. The main Abbeychurch has lovely [?Cotswold] stone. No nave, designed by Hugo from Cluny who was influenced by Mozarabic in Spain. A well-kept old town. We cycled back to the campsite. A boys' camp packed up. Lots more Dutch. Cows. I looked in a cabin while it was being cleaned. The campsite felt airless. During the night it rained + rained.
Saturday August 1st.
It still rained in the morning. 10.30. We set off + had elevenses at the Abbey of Dieu Donné Abbaye [?Bènisitions] Dieu, gorgeous steeple + roof of Beaune, coloured tiles. Benedictine. 1135
pages.notes: Page number 76 visible at top right. The text begins mid-sentence continuing from the left page (overlap). '[?Mouskatère]' is uncertain — could be a supermarket or brand name. '[?Cotswold]' is uncertain — possibly 'coloured' or another descriptor of the stone. '[?Bènisitions]' is unclear — possibly 'Bénédictions' or 'Bénédictins'. 'Abbey of Dieu Donné' may be 'Dieu-Dieu' or another place name. 'Beaune' appears in context of the abbey roof (coloured tiles). The year is not stated on this page; 'Saturday August 1st' could correspond to several years within the 2003–2019 range — cannot confirm without additional context. '1135' likely refers to a founding date.
